title: CLI subprocess test helper can hang the suite indefinitely
reasoning: The main CLI test helper wraps subprocess.run without any timeout, so any regression that blocks in the CLI will block pytest/CI until the outer job times out. The e2e helper for the same CLI command path already uses timeout=180, which shows the intended behavior is to bound subprocess-driven tests.
recommendation: Add a reasonable timeout to tests/test_cli.py::run_mint, matching or intentionally differing from the e2e helper, and include stdout/stderr in timeout failure diagnostics if needed.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests exercise many CLI commands through this helper, but none assert that the helper bounds execution time or fails fast for a hung child process.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a small helper-level test that monkeypatches subprocess.run or invokes a controlled long-running command path and asserts run_mint passes a timeout argument.
minimumFixScope: Update tests/test_cli.py::run_mint to pass timeout=... to subprocess.run.

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. In current tests/test_cli.py, run_mint at lines 16-33 now passes timeout=180 to subprocess.run, matching tests/e2e/test_cli_e2e.py::run_mint. A targeted CLI test using that helper passes, so the specific unbounded subprocess-helper issue is no longer present.
